title: apiv2 tests: refactor complicated curls
body:
Some months ago, apiv2 tests got added that needed new
functionality: passing a tarball to the remote server.
There was no mechanism to do so in the 't' helper, so
these tests used complicated (and actually not-really-
working) curl commands.

This PR introduces and documents a new usage of 't', in
which passing an argument ending in '.tar' adds the
right magic syntax (--data-binary @PATH) to the existing
curl. This lets us use all standard 't' checks, making
for simpler tests and in the process fixing some bugs.

Also: set CONTAINERS_REGISTRIES_CONF when starting server
and when running direct podman, to avoid docker.io throttling.

Signed-off-by: Ed Santiago <santiago@redhat.com>

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *edsantiago* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [edsantiago] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

Link to [test results](https://storage.googleapis.com/cirrus-ci-6707778565701632-fcae48/artifacts/containers/podman/4927841467891712/html/apiv2-podman-fedora-35-root-host.log.html). The `grep` error is gone, but I noticed a new error:
```
     ok 646 [35-networks] POST libpod/networks/create [-d {"name":"network1"}] : .name=network1
     /var/tmp/go/src/github.com/containers/podman/test/apiv2/35-networks.at: line 12: .created~[0-9]\{4\}-[0-9]\{2\}-[0-9]\{2\}.*: command not found
```
Cause is a missing backslash on the previous line, fix is trivial, so I've re-pushed.

Updated [test results](https://storage.googleapis.com/cirrus-ci-6707778565701632-fcae48/artifacts/containers/podman/6011791617556480/html/apiv2-podman-fedora-35-root-host.log.html). The grep error is still gone, as is the network-test one.

LGTM
but I'm hanging on by my fingernails for the `.at` changes.  Not my bailey-wick.
